mission.title: karmada: Proposal: get member clusters resource aggregation by `karmadactl`
mission.description: Proposal: get member clusters resource aggregation by `karmadactl`. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current karmada deployment
steps.description:
Verify your karmada version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
helm list -n karmada 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working karmada installation.

steps.title: Review karmada configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant karmada configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
kubectl get configmap -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=karmada
```
**What would you like to be added**:

Add command for `karmadactl`, such as `karmadactl get pod`, `karmadactl get deploy` to aggregate the pod or deployment resource in member clusters 

**Why is this needed**:

In the management of multi-clusters,

steps.title: Apply the fix for Proposal: get member clusters resource aggregation by…
steps.description:
This PR provides the karmadactl get command, get multi-cluster information via karmadactl.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
kubectl get events -n karmada --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Proposal: get member clusters resource aggregation by…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This PR provides the karmadactl get command, get multi-cluster information via karmadactl.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with karmada installed or the issue environment reproducible.
